chunks.heading: Gizelle Morales

chunks.text: CAUTioN: This message and sender come from outside Pima County. If you did not expect this message, proceed with caution.

## RESOLUTIONOFTHEPIMACOUNTYBOARDOFSUPERVISORS

WHEREAS, the Pima County Board of Supervisors has a duty to uphold constitutional rights, due process protections, and the lawful treatment of all individuals within the United States; and

taken into custody on or about May 18, 2026, by personnel identified as agents of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS); and

WHEREAS,concernshavebeenraised thatsuchdetentionmayhaveoccurred withoutpresentationofa

judicialwarrantorother documentationof lawfulauthority atthetimeof theincident; and

detention, arrest, oruse of force, and that all individuals are presumed to act within lawful authority unlessanduntilacourtfindsotherwise;and

seizure and deprivationof libertywithout dueprocessof law;and

WHEREAS,transparency,accountability, and public trust require that credible allegations of unlawful federalenforcementconductbeindependentlyreviewed byappropriateoversightauthorities; NOW,THEREFORE,BEITRESOLVEDTHAT:

The Pima County Board of Supervisors respectfully requests a formal investigation into the circumstances surrounding the detention of Carla Toledo on or about May 18, 2026, including whether all applicable constitutional, statutory, and procedural requirements were followed by any involved federalofficersoragencies.

The Board urges the United States Departmentof Homeland Security Office of Inspector General, the United States Department of Justice Office of the Inspector General, and any other appropriate federal

oversight body to review this matter for compliance with federal law and internal enforcement policies. The Board requests that, if it is determined through competent legal authority that any individual was detained without lawful authority, that appropriate remedial action be taken, which may include release, correction of records,and any otherremediespermitted bylaw.

may have been violated.

The Clerkof the Board is directed totransmit thisresolutionto:

The Secretaryof Homeland Security
